policy: Use this as the authoritative mob-role map for In Control, Scaling Health, YDM MobHealthBar, Bountiful, skill hooks, and future Ascendant Nameplates. Do not remove mobs to fix balance; reassign role, region, spawn condition, or reward tier first.

groups.healthbar_policy: hidden unless directly targeted by debug tools
groups.runtime_owner: mod configs and vanilla biome rules
vanilla, Improved Mobs, Scaling Health conservative config

groups.spawn_identity: night raids, settlement outskirts, blood moon pressure
groups.runtime_owner: In Control rules planned; Guard Villagers handles defense

groups.spawn_identity: biome-specific danger that makes regions distinct
groups.healthbar_policy: longer visibility after combat; passive variants excluded
groups.runtime_owner: mod biome configs and future In Control regional overrides

groups.healthbar_policy: visible when targeted, damaged, or aggressive; nameplate should show tier later
groups.runtime_owner: Bountiful pools active; spawn review pending

groups.healthbar_policy: Enhanced Boss Bars owns true boss presentation
groups.runtime_owner: mod structures plus generated bounty/skill hook registries

groups.healthbar_policy: boss/elite presentation; never treated as routine hostile
groups.runtime_owner: IceAndFire configs and future Ascendant Encounter Director if distance/rank gating is needed

next_live_rules: Generate In Control rules from this group map after verifying exact entity IDs and biome/dimension targets.
Use mob_registry spawn_review flags to decide which generated mobs need explicit spawn rules.
Use this same group map for future enemy nameplates so passive mobs stay quiet and elite threats stay readable.
